rendered_sections.title: Tool Contracts
rendered_sections.content:
- graph.describe@v1: Payload with selector string
- graph.find@v1: Payload with optional node_class, name_regex, path_regex, logical_key_regex, exported, case_sensitive, and limit
- graph.path@v1: Payload with start selector, end selector, and optional max_hops
- graph.resolve@v1: Payload with selector string
- run_python_query@v1: Payload with code string plus optional bindings, include_export, export_kwargs, and limits
- session.add@v1: Payload with target selector and optional detail of skeleton|summary|neighborhood|source
- session.collapse@v1: Payload with optional target selector and include_descendants boolean.
- session.estimate_expand@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode, depth, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.estimate_hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.event_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.expand_dependencies@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_dependents@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_file@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.explain_export_omission@v1: Payload with optional target selector plus export tuning fields like max_tokens, compact, visible_levels, and class filters.
- session.export@v1: Payload with optional max_tokens, compact, include_rendered, visible_levels, only_node_classes, exclude_node_classes, max_frontier_actions, and max_rendered_bytes
- session.focus@v1: Payload with optional target selector; null or omission clears focus
- session.hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.hydrate_source@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets.
- session.mutation_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.pin@v1: Payload with optional target selector and pinned boolean.
- session.prune@v1: Payload with optional max_selected integer.
- session.recommendations@v1: Payload with optional top integer
- session.walk@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode=file|dependencies|dependents, depth, limit, relation, relations, priority_threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.why@v1: Payload with target selector
- session.why_pruned@v1: Payload with optional target selector.

## Base Runtime Instructions
GraphBench runtime. Stay bounded, typed, and evidence-oriented.

## UCL Command Reference

## Rules
1. Output ONLY the UCL command(s), no explanations or markdown
2. Use exact block IDs as provided
3. String values must be quoted with double quotes
4. Block IDs are short numeric IDs (1, 2, 3, etc.)
5. Do NOT use operators like + for string concatenation - just provide the full value
6. Responses must be schema-valid.

## Response Contract
Return JSON only with this exact shape: {{"kind":"think|tool_call|complete","assistant_message":"<brief status>","tool_call":{{"tool_name":"<one registered tool name>","payload":{{}}}},"acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"],"readiness_state":"not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit"}}. Use null or omit tool_call unless kind is tool_call. When kind is tool_call, tool_call.tool_name must exactly match one of the registered tool names shown below. ## Tool Contracts
- graph.describe@v1: Payload with selector string
- graph.find@v1: Payload with optional node_class, name_regex, path_regex, logical_key_regex, exported, case_sensitive, and limit
- graph.path@v1: Payload with start selector, end selector, and optional max_hops
- graph.resolve@v1: Payload with selector string
- run_python_query@v1: Payload with code string plus optional bindings, include_export, export_kwargs, and limits
- session.add@v1: Payload with target selector and optional detail of skeleton|summary|neighborhood|source
- session.collapse@v1: Payload with optional target selector and include_descendants boolean.
- session.estimate_expand@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode, depth, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.estimate_hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.event_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.expand_dependencies@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_dependents@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_file@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.explain_export_omission@v1: Payload with optional target selector plus export tuning fields like max_tokens, compact, visible_levels, and class filters.
- session.export@v1: Payload with optional max_tokens, compact, include_rendered, visible_levels, only_node_classes, exclude_node_classes, max_frontier_actions, and max_rendered_bytes
- session.focus@v1: Payload with optional target selector; null or omission clears focus
- session.hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.hydrate_source@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets.
- session.mutation_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.pin@v1: Payload with optional target selector and pinned boolean.
- session.prune@v1: Payload with optional max_selected integer.
- session.recommendations@v1: Payload with optional top integer
- session.walk@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode=file|dependencies|dependents, depth, limit, relation, relations, priority_threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.why@v1: Payload with target selector
- session.why_pruned@v1: Payload with optional target selector.

rendered_sections.title: Tool Contracts
rendered_sections.content:
- graph.describe@v1: Payload with selector string
- graph.find@v1: Payload with optional node_class, name_regex, path_regex, logical_key_regex, exported, case_sensitive, and limit
- graph.path@v1: Payload with start selector, end selector, and optional max_hops
- graph.resolve@v1: Payload with selector string
- run_python_query@v1: Payload with code string plus optional bindings, include_export, export_kwargs, and limits
- session.add@v1: Payload with target selector and optional detail of skeleton|summary|neighborhood|source
- session.collapse@v1: Payload with optional target selector and include_descendants boolean.
- session.estimate_expand@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode, depth, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.estimate_hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.event_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.expand_dependencies@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_dependents@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_file@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.explain_export_omission@v1: Payload with optional target selector plus export tuning fields like max_tokens, compact, visible_levels, and class filters.
- session.export@v1: Payload with optional max_tokens, compact, include_rendered, visible_levels, only_node_classes, exclude_node_classes, max_frontier_actions, and max_rendered_bytes
- session.focus@v1: Payload with optional target selector; null or omission clears focus
- session.hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.hydrate_source@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets.
- session.mutation_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.pin@v1: Payload with optional target selector and pinned boolean.
- session.prune@v1: Payload with optional max_selected integer.
- session.recommendations@v1: Payload with optional top integer
- session.walk@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode=file|dependencies|dependents, depth, limit, relation, relations, priority_threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.why@v1: Payload with target selector
- session.why_pruned@v1: Payload with optional target selector.

## Tool Contracts
- graph.describe@v1: Payload with selector string
- graph.find@v1: Payload with optional node_class, name_regex, path_regex, logical_key_regex, exported, case_sensitive, and limit
- graph.path@v1: Payload with start selector, end selector, and optional max_hops
- graph.resolve@v1: Payload with selector string
- run_python_query@v1: Payload with code string plus optional bindings, include_export, export_kwargs, and limits
- session.add@v1: Payload with target selector and optional detail of skeleton|summary|neighborhood|source
- session.collapse@v1: Payload with optional target selector and include_descendants boolean.
- session.estimate_expand@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode, depth, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.estimate_hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.event_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.expand_dependencies@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_dependents@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_file@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.explain_export_omission@v1: Payload with optional target selector plus export tuning fields like max_tokens, compact, visible_levels, and class filters.
- session.export@v1: Payload with optional max_tokens, compact, include_rendered, visible_levels, only_node_classes, exclude_node_classes, max_frontier_actions, and max_rendered_bytes
- session.focus@v1: Payload with optional target selector; null or omission clears focus
- session.hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.hydrate_source@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets.
- session.mutation_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.pin@v1: Payload with optional target selector and pinned boolean.
- session.prune@v1: Payload with optional max_selected integer.
- session.recommendations@v1: Payload with optional top integer
- session.walk@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode=file|dependencies|dependents, depth, limit, relation, relations, priority_threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.why@v1: Payload with target selector
- session.why_pruned@v1: Payload with optional target selector.

rendered_sections.title: Base Runtime Instructions
rendered_sections.content:
GraphBench runtime. Stay bounded, typed, and evidence-oriented.

## UCL Command Reference

## Rules
1. Output ONLY the UCL command(s), no explanations or markdown
2. Use exact block IDs as provided
3. String values must be quoted with double quotes
4. Block IDs are short numeric IDs (1, 2, 3, etc.)
5. Do NOT use operators like + for string concatenation - just provide the full value
6. Responses must be schema-valid.

rendered_sections.title: Response Contract
rendered_sections.content: Return JSON only with this exact shape: {{"kind":"think|tool_call|complete","assistant_message":"<brief status>","tool_call":{{"tool_name":"<one registered tool name>","payload":{{}}}},"acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"],"readiness_state":"not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit"}}. Use null or omit tool_call unless kind is tool_call. When kind is tool_call, tool_call.tool_name must exactly match one of the registered tool names shown below. Do not invent helper tools, verifier tools, or meta tools.

rendered_sections.title: Tool Contracts
rendered_sections.content:
- graph.describe@v1: Payload with selector string
- graph.find@v1: Payload with optional node_class, name_regex, path_regex, logical_key_regex, exported, case_sensitive, and limit
- graph.path@v1: Payload with start selector, end selector, and optional max_hops
- graph.resolve@v1: Payload with selector string
- run_python_query@v1: Payload with code string plus optional bindings, include_export, export_kwargs, and limits
- session.add@v1: Payload with target selector and optional detail of skeleton|summary|neighborhood|source
- session.collapse@v1: Payload with optional target selector and include_descendants boolean.
- session.estimate_expand@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode, depth, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.estimate_hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.event_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.expand_dependencies@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_dependents@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_file@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.explain_export_omission@v1: Payload with optional target selector plus export tuning fields like max_tokens, compact, visible_levels, and class filters.
- session.export@v1: Payload with optional max_tokens, compact, include_rendered, visible_levels, only_node_classes, exclude_node_classes, max_frontier_actions, and max_rendered_bytes
- session.focus@v1: Payload with optional target selector; null or omission clears focus
- session.hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.hydrate_source@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets.
- session.mutation_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.pin@v1: Payload with optional target selector and pinned boolean.
- session.prune@v1: Payload with optional max_selected integer.
- session.recommendations@v1: Payload with optional top integer
- session.walk@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode=file|dependencies|dependents, depth, limit, relation, relations, priority_threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.why@v1: Payload with target selector
- session.why_pruned@v1: Payload with optional target selector.

turn_trace.readiness_reason: Hydrating source for artifacts.rs to inspect fixture manifest details

entries.rendered_prompt:
## Base Runtime Instructions
GraphBench runtime. Stay bounded, typed, and evidence-oriented.

## UCL Command Reference

## Rules
1. Output ONLY the UCL command(s), no explanations or markdown
2. Use exact block IDs as provided
3. String values must be quoted with double quotes
4. Block IDs are short numeric IDs (1, 2, 3, etc.)
5. Do NOT use operators like + for string concatenation - just provide the full value
6. Responses must be schema-valid.

## Response Contract
Return JSON only with this exact shape: {{"kind":"think|tool_call|complete","assistant_message":"<brief status>","tool_call":{{"tool_name":"<one registered tool name>","payload":{{}}}},"acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"],"readiness_state":"not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit"}}. Use null or omit tool_call unless kind is tool_call. When kind is tool_call, tool_call.tool_name must exactly match one of the registered tool names shown below. Do not invent helper tools, verifier tools, or meta tools.

## Objective State
Task: Gather the evidence required to edit fixture loading while preserving pinned commit validation, local path resolution, and graph snapshot identity checks.
Class: prepare_to_edit

rendered_sections.title: Base Runtime Instructions
rendered_sections.content:
GraphBench runtime. Stay bounded, typed, and evidence-oriented.

## UCL Command Reference

## Rules
1. Output ONLY the UCL command(s), no explanations or markdown
2. Use exact block IDs as provided
3. String values must be quoted with double quotes
4. Block IDs are short numeric IDs (1, 2, 3, etc.)
5. Do NOT use operators like + for string concatenation - just provide the full value
6. Responses must be schema-valid.

rendered_sections.title: Response Contract
rendered_sections.content: Return JSON only with this exact shape: {{"kind":"think|tool_call|complete","assistant_message":"<brief status>","tool_call":{{"tool_name":"<one registered tool name>","payload":{{}}}},"acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"],"readiness_state":"not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit"}}. Use null or omit tool_call unless kind is tool_call. When kind is tool_call, tool_call.tool_name must exactly match one of the registered tool names shown below. Do not invent helper tools, verifier tools, or meta tools.

rendered_sections.title: Tool Contracts
rendered_sections.content:
- graph.describe@v1: Payload with selector string
- graph.find@v1: Payload with optional node_class, name_regex, path_regex, logical_key_regex, exported, case_sensitive, and limit
- graph.path@v1: Payload with start selector, end selector, and optional max_hops
- graph.resolve@v1: Payload with selector string
- run_python_query@v1: Payload with code string plus optional bindings, include_export, export_kwargs, and limits
- session.add@v1: Payload with target selector and optional detail of skeleton|summary|neighborhood|source
- session.collapse@v1: Payload with optional target selector and include_descendants boolean.
- session.estimate_expand@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode, depth, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.estimate_hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.event_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.expand_dependencies@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_dependents@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_file@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.explain_export_omission@v1: Payload with optional target selector plus export tuning fields like max_tokens, compact, visible_levels, and class filters.
- session.export@v1: Payload with optional max_tokens, compact, include_rendered, visible_levels, only_node_classes, exclude_node_classes, max_frontier_actions, and max_rendered_bytes
- session.focus@v1: Payload with optional target selector; null or omission clears focus
- session.hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.hydrate_source@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets.
- session.mutation_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.pin@v1: Payload with optional target selector and pinned boolean.
- session.prune@v1: Payload with optional max_selected integer.
- session.recommendations@v1: Payload with optional top integer
- session.walk@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode=file|dependencies|dependents, depth, limit, relation, relations, priority_threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.why@v1: Payload with target selector
- session.why_pruned@v1: Payload with optional target selector.

## Base Runtime Instructions
GraphBench runtime. Stay bounded, typed, and evidence-oriented.

## UCL Command Reference

## Rules
1. Output ONLY the UCL command(s), no explanations or markdown
2. Use exact block IDs as provided
3. String values must be quoted with double quotes
4. Block IDs are short numeric IDs (1, 2, 3, etc.)
5. Do NOT use operators like + for string concatenation - just provide the full value
6. Responses must be schema-valid.

## Response Contract
Return JSON only with this exact shape: {{"kind":"think|tool_call|complete","assistant_message":"<brief status>","tool_call":{{"tool_name":"<one registered tool name>","payload":{{}}}},"acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"],"readiness_state":"not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit"}}. Use null or omit tool_call unless kind is tool_call. When kind is tool_call, tool_call.tool_name must exactly match one of the registered tool names shown below. ## Tool Contracts
- graph.describe@v1: Payload with selector string
- graph.find@v1: Payload with optional node_class, name_regex, path_regex, logical_key_regex, exported, case_sensitive, and limit
- graph.path@v1: Payload with start selector, end selector, and optional max_hops
- graph.resolve@v1: Payload with selector string
- run_python_query@v1: Payload with code string plus optional bindings, include_export, export_kwargs, and limits
- session.add@v1: Payload with target selector and optional detail of skeleton|summary|neighborhood|source
- session.collapse@v1: Payload with optional target selector and include_descendants boolean.
- session.estimate_expand@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode, depth, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.estimate_hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.event_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.expand_dependencies@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_dependents@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_file@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.explain_export_omission@v1: Payload with optional target selector plus export tuning fields like max_tokens, compact, visible_levels, and class filters.
- session.export@v1: Payload with optional max_tokens, compact, include_rendered, visible_levels, only_node_classes, exclude_node_classes, max_frontier_actions, and max_rendered_bytes
- session.focus@v1: Payload with optional target selector; null or omission clears focus
- session.hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.hydrate_source@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets.
- session.mutation_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.pin@v1: Payload with optional target selector and pinned boolean.
- session.prune@v1: Payload with optional max_selected integer.
- session.recommendations@v1: Payload with optional top integer
- session.walk@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode=file|dependencies|dependents, depth, limit, relation, relations, priority_threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.why@v1: Payload with target selector
- session.why_pruned@v1: Payload with optional target selector.

## Tool Contracts
- graph.describe@v1: Payload with selector string
- graph.find@v1: Payload with optional node_class, name_regex, path_regex, logical_key_regex, exported, case_sensitive, and limit
- graph.path@v1: Payload with start selector, end selector, and optional max_hops
- graph.resolve@v1: Payload with selector string
- run_python_query@v1: Payload with code string plus optional bindings, include_export, export_kwargs, and limits
- session.add@v1: Payload with target selector and optional detail of skeleton|summary|neighborhood|source
- session.collapse@v1: Payload with optional target selector and include_descendants boolean.
- session.estimate_expand@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode, depth, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.estimate_hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.event_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.expand_dependencies@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_dependents@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_file@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.explain_export_omission@v1: Payload with optional target selector plus export tuning fields like max_tokens, compact, visible_levels, and class filters.
- session.export@v1: Payload with optional max_tokens, compact, include_rendered, visible_levels, only_node_classes, exclude_node_classes, max_frontier_actions, and max_rendered_bytes
- session.focus@v1: Payload with optional target selector; null or omission clears focus
- session.hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.hydrate_source@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets.
- session.mutation_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.pin@v1: Payload with optional target selector and pinned boolean.
- session.prune@v1: Payload with optional max_selected integer.
- session.recommendations@v1: Payload with optional top integer
- session.walk@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode=file|dependencies|dependents, depth, limit, relation, relations, priority_threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.why@v1: Payload with target selector
- session.why_pruned@v1: Payload with optional target selector.

rendered_sections.title: Base Runtime Instructions
rendered_sections.content:
GraphBench runtime. Stay bounded, typed, and evidence-oriented.

## UCL Command Reference

## Rules
1. Output ONLY the UCL command(s), no explanations or markdown
2. Use exact block IDs as provided
3. String values must be quoted with double quotes
4. Block IDs are short numeric IDs (1, 2, 3, etc.)
5. Do NOT use operators like + for string concatenation - just provide the full value
6. Responses must be schema-valid.

rendered_sections.title: Response Contract
rendered_sections.content: Return JSON only with this exact shape: {{"kind":"think|tool_call|complete","assistant_message":"<brief status>","tool_call":{{"tool_name":"<one registered tool name>","payload":{{}}}},"acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"],"readiness_state":"not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit"}}. Use null or omit tool_call unless kind is tool_call. When kind is tool_call, tool_call.tool_name must exactly match one of the registered tool names shown below. Do not invent helper tools, verifier tools, or meta tools.

rendered_sections.title: Tool Contracts
rendered_sections.content:
- graph.describe@v1: Payload with selector string
- graph.find@v1: Payload with optional node_class, name_regex, path_regex, logical_key_regex, exported, case_sensitive, and limit
- graph.path@v1: Payload with start selector, end selector, and optional max_hops
- graph.resolve@v1: Payload with selector string
- run_python_query@v1: Payload with code string plus optional bindings, include_export, export_kwargs, and limits
- session.add@v1: Payload with target selector and optional detail of skeleton|summary|neighborhood|source
- session.collapse@v1: Payload with optional target selector and include_descendants boolean.
- session.estimate_expand@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode, depth, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.estimate_hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.event_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.expand_dependencies@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_dependents@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_file@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.explain_export_omission@v1: Payload with optional target selector plus export tuning fields like max_tokens, compact, visible_levels, and class filters.
- session.export@v1: Payload with optional max_tokens, compact, include_rendered, visible_levels, only_node_classes, exclude_node_classes, max_frontier_actions, and max_rendered_bytes
- session.focus@v1: Payload with optional target selector; null or omission clears focus
- session.hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.hydrate_source@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets.
- session.mutation_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.pin@v1: Payload with optional target selector and pinned boolean.
- session.prune@v1: Payload with optional max_selected integer.
- session.recommendations@v1: Payload with optional top integer
- session.walk@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode=file|dependencies|dependents, depth, limit, relation, relations, priority_threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.why@v1: Payload with target selector
- session.why_pruned@v1: Payload with optional target selector.

entries.rendered_prompt:
## Base Runtime Instructions
GraphBench runtime. Stay bounded, typed, and evidence-oriented.

## UCL Command Reference

## Rules
1. Output ONLY the UCL command(s), no explanations or markdown
2. Use exact block IDs as provided
3. String values must be quoted with double quotes
4. Block IDs are short numeric IDs (1, 2, 3, etc.)
5. Do NOT use operators like + for string concatenation - just provide the full value
6. Responses must be schema-valid.

## Response Contract
Return JSON only with this exact shape: {{"kind":"think|tool_call|complete","assistant_message":"<brief status>","tool_call":{{"tool_name":"<one registered tool name>","payload":{{}}}},"acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"],"readiness_state":"not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit"}}. Use null or omit tool_call unless kind is tool_call. When kind is tool_call, tool_call.tool_name must exactly match one of the registered tool names shown below. Do not invent helper tools, verifier tools, or meta tools.

## Tool Contracts
- graph.describe@v1: Payload with selector string
- graph.find@v1: Payload with optional node_class, name_regex, path_regex, logical_key_regex, exported, case_sensitive, and limit
- graph.path@v1: Payload with start selector, end selector, and optional max_hops
- graph.resolve@v1: Payload with selector string
- run_python_query@v1: Payload with code string plus optional bindings, include_export, export_kwargs, and limits
- session.add@v1: Payload with target selector and optional detail of skeleton|summary|neighborhood|source
- session.collapse@v1: Payload with optional target selector and include_descendants boolean.
- session.estimate_expand@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode, depth, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.estimate_hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.event_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.expand_dependencies@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_dependents@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_file@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.explain_export_omission@v1: Payload with optional target selector plus export tuning fields like max_tokens, compact, visible_levels, and class filters.
- session.export@v1: Payload with optional max_tokens, compact, include_rendered, visible_levels, only_node_classes, exclude_node_classes, max_frontier_actions, and max_rendered_bytes
- session.focus@v1: Payload with optional target selector; null or omission clears focus
- session.hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.hydrate_source@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets.
- session.mutation_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.pin@v1: Payload with optional target selector and pinned boolean.
- session.prune@v1: Payload with optional max_selected integer.
- session.recommendations@v1: Payload with optional top integer
- session.walk@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode=file|dependencies|dependents, depth, limit, relation, relations, priority_threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.why@v1: Payload with target selector
- session.why_pruned@v1: Payload with optional target selector.

rendered_sections.title: Base Runtime Instructions
rendered_sections.content:
GraphBench runtime. Stay bounded, typed, and evidence-oriented.

## UCL Command Reference

## Rules
1. Output ONLY the UCL command(s), no explanations or markdown
2. Use exact block IDs as provided
3. String values must be quoted with double quotes
4. Block IDs are short numeric IDs (1, 2, 3, etc.)
5. Do NOT use operators like + for string concatenation - just provide the full value
6. Responses must be schema-valid.

rendered_sections.title: Response Contract
rendered_sections.content: Return JSON only with this exact shape: {{"kind":"think|tool_call|complete","assistant_message":"<brief status>","tool_call":{{"tool_name":"<one registered tool name>","payload":{{}}}},"acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"],"readiness_state":"not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit"}}. Use null or omit tool_call unless kind is tool_call. When kind is tool_call, tool_call.tool_name must exactly match one of the registered tool names shown below. Do not invent helper tools, verifier tools, or meta tools.

rendered_sections.title: Tool Contracts
rendered_sections.content:
- graph.describe@v1: Payload with selector string
- graph.find@v1: Payload with optional node_class, name_regex, path_regex, logical_key_regex, exported, case_sensitive, and limit
- graph.path@v1: Payload with start selector, end selector, and optional max_hops
- graph.resolve@v1: Payload with selector string
- run_python_query@v1: Payload with code string plus optional bindings, include_export, export_kwargs, and limits
- session.add@v1: Payload with target selector and optional detail of skeleton|summary|neighborhood|source
- session.collapse@v1: Payload with optional target selector and include_descendants boolean.
- session.estimate_expand@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode, depth, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.estimate_hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.event_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.expand_dependencies@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_dependents@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_file@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.explain_export_omission@v1: Payload with optional target selector plus export tuning fields like max_tokens, compact, visible_levels, and class filters.
- session.export@v1: Payload with optional max_tokens, compact, include_rendered, visible_levels, only_node_classes, exclude_node_classes, max_frontier_actions, and max_rendered_bytes
- session.focus@v1: Payload with optional target selector; null or omission clears focus
- session.hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.hydrate_source@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets.
- session.mutation_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.pin@v1: Payload with optional target selector and pinned boolean.
- session.prune@v1: Payload with optional max_selected integer.
- session.recommendations@v1: Payload with optional top integer
- session.walk@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode=file|dependencies|dependents, depth, limit, relation, relations, priority_threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.why@v1: Payload with target selector
- session.why_pruned@v1: Payload with optional target selector.

## Base Runtime Instructions

## Rules

5. Do NOT use operators like + for string concatenation - just provide the full value

rendered_sections.content:
GraphBench runtime. Stay bounded, typed, and evidence-oriented.

## UCL Command Reference

## Rules
1. Output ONLY the UCL command(s), no explanations or markdown
2. Use exact block IDs as provided
3. String values must be quoted with double quotes
4. Block IDs are short numeric IDs (1, 2, 3, etc.)
5. Do NOT use operators like + for string concatenation - just provide the full value

rendered_sections.title: Tool Contracts
rendered_sections.content:
- graph.describe@v1: Payload with selector string
- graph.find@v1: Payload with optional node_class, name_regex, path_regex, logical_key_regex, exported, case_sensitive, and limit
- graph.path@v1: Payload with start selector, end selector, and optional max_hops
- graph.resolve@v1: Payload with selector string
- run_python_query@v1: Payload with code string plus optional bindings, include_export, export_kwargs, and limits
- session.add@v1: Payload with target selector and optional detail of skeleton|summary|neighborhood|source
- session.collapse@v1: Payload with optional target selector and include_descendants boolean.
- session.estimate_expand@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode, depth, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.estimate_hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.event_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.expand_dependencies@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_dependents@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_file@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.explain_export_omission@v1: Payload with optional target selector plus export tuning fields like max_tokens, compact, visible_levels, and class filters.
- session.export@v1: Payload with optional max_tokens, compact, include_rendered, visible_levels, only_node_classes, exclude_node_classes, max_frontier_actions, and max_rendered_bytes
- session.focus@v1: Payload with optional target selector; null or omission clears focus
- session.hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.hydrate_source@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets.
- session.mutation_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.pin@v1: Payload with optional target selector and pinned boolean.
- session.prune@v1: Payload with optional max_selected integer.
- session.recommendations@v1: Payload with optional top integer
- session.walk@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode=file|dependencies|dependents, depth, limit, relation, relations, priority_threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.why@v1: Payload with target selector
- session.why_pruned@v1: Payload with optional target selector.

GraphBench runtime. Stay bounded, typed, and evidence-oriented.

## UCL Command Reference

## Rules
1. Output ONLY the UCL command(s), no explanations or markdown

3. String values must be quoted with double quotes
4. Block IDs are short numeric IDs (1, 2, 3, etc.)

## Tool Contracts
- graph.describe@v1: Payload with selector string
- graph.find@v1: Payload with optional node_class, name_regex, path_regex, logical_key_regex, exported, case_sensitive, and limit
- graph.path@v1: Payload with start selector, end selector, and optional max_hops
- graph.resolve@v1: Payload with selector string
- run_python_query@v1: Payload with code string plus optional bindings, include_export, export_kwargs, and limits
- session.add@v1: Payload with target selector and optional detail of skeleton|summary|neighborhood|source
- session.collapse@v1: Payload with optional target selector and include_descendants boolean.
- session.estimate_expand@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode, depth, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.estimate_hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.event_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.expand_dependencies@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_dependents@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_file@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.explain_export_omission@v1: Payload with optional target selector plus export tuning fields like max_tokens, compact, visible_levels, and class filters.
- session.export@v1: Payload with optional max_tokens, compact, include_rendered, visible_levels, only_node_classes, exclude_node_classes, max_frontier_actions, and max_rendered_bytes
- session.focus@v1: Payload with optional target selector; null or omission clears focus
- session.hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.hydrate_source@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets.
- session.mutation_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.pin@v1: Payload with optional target selector and pinned boolean.
- session.prune@v1: Payload with optional max_selected integer.
- session.recommendations@v1: Payload with optional top integer
- session.walk@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode=file|dependencies|dependents, depth, limit, relation, relations, priority_threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.why@v1: Payload with target selector
- session.why_pruned@v1: Payload with optional target selector.

rendered_sections.title: Base Runtime Instructions
rendered_sections.content:
GraphBench runtime. Stay bounded, typed, and evidence-oriented.

## UCL Command Reference

## Rules
1. Output ONLY the UCL command(s), no explanations or markdown
2. Use exact block IDs as provided
3. String values must be quoted with double quotes
4. Block IDs are short numeric IDs (1, 2, 3, etc.)
5. Do NOT use operators like + for string concatenation - just provide the full value
6. Responses must be schema-valid.

rendered_sections.title: Response Contract
rendered_sections.content: Return JSON only with this exact shape: {{"kind":"think|tool_call|complete","assistant_message":"<brief status>","tool_call":{{"tool_name":"<one registered tool name>","payload":{{}}}},"acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"],"readiness_state":"not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit"}}. Use null or omit tool_call unless kind is tool_call. When kind is tool_call, tool_call.tool_name must exactly match one of the registered tool names shown below. Do not invent helper tools, verifier tools, or meta tools.

rendered_sections.title: Tool Contracts
rendered_sections.content:
- graph.describe@v1: Payload with selector string
- graph.find@v1: Payload with optional node_class, name_regex, path_regex, logical_key_regex, exported, case_sensitive, and limit
- graph.path@v1: Payload with start selector, end selector, and optional max_hops
- graph.resolve@v1: Payload with selector string
- run_python_query@v1: Payload with code string plus optional bindings, include_export, export_kwargs, and limits
- session.add@v1: Payload with target selector and optional detail of skeleton|summary|neighborhood|source
- session.collapse@v1: Payload with optional target selector and include_descendants boolean.
- session.estimate_expand@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode, depth, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.estimate_hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.event_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.expand_dependencies@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_dependents@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_file@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.explain_export_omission@v1: Payload with optional target selector plus export tuning fields like max_tokens, compact, visible_levels, and class filters.
- session.export@v1: Payload with optional max_tokens, compact, include_rendered, visible_levels, only_node_classes, exclude_node_classes, max_frontier_actions, and max_rendered_bytes
- session.focus@v1: Payload with optional target selector; null or omission clears focus
- session.hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.hydrate_source@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets.
- session.mutation_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.pin@v1: Payload with optional target selector and pinned boolean.
- session.prune@v1: Payload with optional max_selected integer.
- session.recommendations@v1: Payload with optional top integer
- session.walk@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode=file|dependencies|dependents, depth, limit, relation, relations, priority_threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.why@v1: Payload with target selector
- session.why_pruned@v1: Payload with optional target selector.

entries.rendered_prompt:
## Base Runtime Instructions
GraphBench runtime. Stay bounded, typed, and evidence-oriented.

## UCL Command Reference

## Rules
1. Output ONLY the UCL command(s), no explanations or markdown
2. Use exact block IDs as provided
3. String values must be quoted with double quotes
4. Block IDs are short numeric IDs (1, 2, 3, etc.)
5. Do NOT use operators like + for string concatenation - just provide the full value
6. Responses must be schema-valid.

## Response Contract
Return JSON only with this exact shape: {{"kind":"think|tool_call|complete","assistant_message":"<brief status>","tool_call":{{"tool_name":"<one registered tool name>","payload":{{}}}},"acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"],"readiness_state":"not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit"}}. Use null or omit tool_call unless kind is tool_call. When kind is tool_call, tool_call.tool_name must exactly match one of the registered tool names shown below. Do not invent helper tools, verifier tools, or meta tools.

## Tool Contracts
- graph.describe@v1: Payload with selector string
- graph.find@v1: Payload with optional node_class, name_regex, path_regex, logical_key_regex, exported, case_sensitive, and limit
- graph.path@v1: Payload with start selector, end selector, and optional max_hops
- graph.resolve@v1: Payload with selector string
- run_python_query@v1: Payload with code string plus optional bindings, include_export, export_kwargs, and limits
- session.add@v1: Payload with target selector and optional detail of skeleton|summary|neighborhood|source
- session.collapse@v1: Payload with optional target selector and include_descendants boolean.
- session.estimate_expand@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode, depth, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.estimate_hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.event_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.expand_dependencies@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_dependents@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_file@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.explain_export_omission@v1: Payload with optional target selector plus export tuning fields like max_tokens, compact, visible_levels, and class filters.
- session.export@v1: Payload with optional max_tokens, compact, include_rendered, visible_levels, only_node_classes, exclude_node_classes, max_frontier_actions, and max_rendered_bytes
- session.focus@v1: Payload with optional target selector; null or omission clears focus
- session.hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.hydrate_source@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets.
- session.mutation_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.pin@v1: Payload with optional target selector and pinned boolean.
- session.prune@v1: Payload with optional max_selected integer.
- session.recommendations@v1: Payload with optional top integer
- session.walk@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode=file|dependencies|dependents, depth, limit, relation, relations, priority_threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.why@v1: Payload with target selector
- session.why_pruned@v1: Payload with optional target selector.

rendered_sections.title: Base Runtime Instructions
rendered_sections.content:
GraphBench runtime. Stay bounded, typed, and evidence-oriented.

## UCL Command Reference

## Rules
1. Output ONLY the UCL command(s), no explanations or markdown
2. Use exact block IDs as provided
3. String values must be quoted with double quotes
4. Block IDs are short numeric IDs (1, 2, 3, etc.)
5. Do NOT use operators like + for string concatenation - just provide the full value
6. Responses must be schema-valid.

rendered_sections.title: Response Contract
rendered_sections.content: Return JSON only with this exact shape: {{"kind":"think|tool_call|complete","assistant_message":"<brief status>","tool_call":{{"tool_name":"<one registered tool name>","payload":{{}}}},"acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"],"readiness_state":"not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit"}}. Use null or omit tool_call unless kind is tool_call. When kind is tool_call, tool_call.tool_name must exactly match one of the registered tool names shown below. Do not invent helper tools, verifier tools, or meta tools.

rendered_sections.title: Tool Contracts
rendered_sections.content:
- graph.describe@v1: Payload with selector string
- graph.find@v1: Payload with optional node_class, name_regex, path_regex, logical_key_regex, exported, case_sensitive, and limit
- graph.path@v1: Payload with start selector, end selector, and optional max_hops
- graph.resolve@v1: Payload with selector string
- run_python_query@v1: Payload with code string plus optional bindings, include_export, export_kwargs, and limits
- session.add@v1: Payload with target selector and optional detail of skeleton|summary|neighborhood|source
- session.collapse@v1: Payload with optional target selector and include_descendants boolean.
- session.estimate_expand@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode, depth, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.estimate_hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.event_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.expand_dependencies@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_dependents@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_file@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.explain_export_omission@v1: Payload with optional target selector plus export tuning fields like max_tokens, compact, visible_levels, and class filters.
- session.export@v1: Payload with optional max_tokens, compact, include_rendered, visible_levels, only_node_classes, exclude_node_classes, max_frontier_actions, and max_rendered_bytes
- session.focus@v1: Payload with optional target selector; null or omission clears focus
- session.hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.hydrate_source@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets.
- session.mutation_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.pin@v1: Payload with optional target selector and pinned boolean.
- session.prune@v1: Payload with optional max_selected integer.
- session.recommendations@v1: Payload with optional top integer
- session.walk@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode=file|dependencies|dependents, depth, limit, relation, relations, priority_threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.why@v1: Payload with target selector
- session.why_pruned@v1: Payload with optional target selector.

entries.rendered_prompt:
## Base Runtime Instructions
GraphBench runtime. Stay bounded, typed, and evidence-oriented.

## UCL Command Reference

## Rules
1. Output ONLY the UCL command(s), no explanations or markdown
2. Use exact block IDs as provided
3. String values must be quoted with double quotes
4. Block IDs are short numeric IDs (1, 2, 3, etc.)
5. Do NOT use operators like + for string concatenation - just provide the full value
6. Responses must be schema-valid.

## Response Contract
Return JSON only with this exact shape: {{"kind":"think|tool_call|complete","assistant_message":"<brief status>","tool_call":{{"tool_name":"<one registered tool name>","payload":{{}}}},"acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"],"readiness_state":"not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit"}}. Use null or omit tool_call unless kind is tool_call. When kind is tool_call, tool_call.tool_name must exactly match one of the registered tool names shown below. Do not invent helper tools, verifier tools, or meta tools.

## Tool Contracts
- graph.describe@v1: Payload with selector string
- graph.find@v1: Payload with optional node_class, name_regex, path_regex, logical_key_regex, exported, case_sensitive, and limit
- graph.path@v1: Payload with start selector, end selector, and optional max_hops
- graph.resolve@v1: Payload with selector string
- run_python_query@v1: Payload with code string plus optional bindings, include_export, export_kwargs, and limits
- session.add@v1: Payload with target selector and optional detail of skeleton|summary|neighborhood|source
- session.collapse@v1: Payload with optional target selector and include_descendants boolean.
- session.estimate_expand@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode, depth, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.estimate_hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.event_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.expand_dependencies@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_dependents@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_file@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.explain_export_omission@v1: Payload with optional target selector plus export tuning fields like max_tokens, compact, visible_levels, and class filters.
- session.export@v1: Payload with optional max_tokens, compact, include_rendered, visible_levels, only_node_classes, exclude_node_classes, max_frontier_actions, and max_rendered_bytes
- session.focus@v1: Payload with optional target selector; null or omission clears focus
- session.hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.hydrate_source@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets.
- session.mutation_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.pin@v1: Payload with optional target selector and pinned boolean.
- session.prune@v1: Payload with optional max_selected integer.
- session.recommendations@v1: Payload with optional top integer
- session.walk@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode=file|dependencies|dependents, depth, limit, relation, relations, priority_threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.why@v1: Payload with target selector
- session.why_pruned@v1: Payload with optional target selector.

rendered_sections.title: Tool Contracts

- graph.describe@v1: Payload with selector string
- graph.find@v1: Payload with optional node_class, name_regex, path_regex, logical_key_regex, exported, case_sensitive, and limit
- graph.path@v1: Payload with start selector, end selector, and optional max_hops
- graph.resolve@v1: Payload with selector string
- run_python_query@v1: Payload with code string plus optional bindings, include_export, export_kwargs, and limits
- session.add@v1: Payload with target selector and optional detail of skeleton|summary|neighborhood|source
- session.collapse@v1: Payload with optional target selector and include_descendants boolean.
- session.estimate_expand@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode, depth, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.estimate_hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.event_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.expand_dependencies@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_dependents@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_file@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.explain_export_omission@v1: Payload with optional target selector plus export tuning fields like max_tokens, compact, visible_levels, and class filters.
- session.export@v1: Payload with optional max_tokens, compact, include_rendered, visible_levels, only_node_classes, exclude_node_classes, max_frontier_actions, and max_rendered_bytes
- session.focus@v1: Payload with optional target selector; null or omission clears focus
- session.hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.hydrate_source@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets.
- session.mutation_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.pin@v1: Payload with optional target selector and pinned boolean.
- session.prune@v1: Payload with optional max_selected integer.
- session.recommendations@v1: Payload with optional top integer
- session.walk@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode=file|dependencies|dependents, depth, limit, relation, relations, priority_threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.why@v1: Payload with target selector
- session.why_pruned@v1: Payload with optional target selector.

## Tool Contracts
- graph.describe@v1: Payload with selector string
- graph.find@v1: Payload with optional node_class, name_regex, path_regex, logical_key_regex, exported, case_sensitive, and limit
- graph.path@v1: Payload with start selector, end selector, and optional max_hops
- graph.resolve@v1: Payload with selector string
- run_python_query@v1: Payload with code string plus optional bindings, include_export, export_kwargs, and limits
- session.add@v1: Payload with target selector and optional detail of skeleton|summary|neighborhood|source
- session.collapse@v1: Payload with optional target selector and include_descendants boolean.
- session.estimate_expand@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode, depth, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.estimate_hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.event_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.expand_dependencies@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_dependents@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_file@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.explain_export_omission@v1: Payload with optional target selector plus export tuning fields like max_tokens, compact, visible_levels, and class filters.
- session.export@v1: Payload with optional max_tokens, compact, include_rendered, visible_levels, only_node_classes, exclude_node_classes, max_frontier_actions, and max_rendered_bytes
- session.focus@v1: Payload with optional target selector; null or omission clears focus
- session.hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.hydrate_source@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets.
- session.mutation_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.pin@v1: Payload with optional target selector and pinned boolean.
- session.prune@v1: Payload with optional max_selected integer.
- session.recommendations@v1: Payload with optional top integer
- session.walk@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode=file|dependencies|dependents, depth, limit, relation, relations, priority_threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.why@v1: Payload with target selector
- session.why_pruned@v1: Payload with optional target selector.

rendered_sections.title: Base Runtime Instructions
rendered_sections.content:
GraphBench runtime. Stay bounded, typed, and evidence-oriented.

## UCL Command Reference

## Rules
1. Output ONLY the UCL command(s), no explanations or markdown
2. Use exact block IDs as provided
3. String values must be quoted with double quotes
4. Block IDs are short numeric IDs (1, 2, 3, etc.)
5. Do NOT use operators like + for string concatenation - just provide the full value
6. Responses must be schema-valid.

rendered_sections.title: Response Contract
rendered_sections.content: Return JSON only with this exact shape: {{"kind":"think|tool_call|complete","assistant_message":"<brief status>","tool_call":{{"tool_name":"<one registered tool name>","payload":{{}}}},"acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"],"readiness_state":"not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit"}}. Use null or omit tool_call unless kind is tool_call. When kind is tool_call, tool_call.tool_name must exactly match one of the registered tool names shown below. Do not invent helper tools, verifier tools, or meta tools.

rendered_sections.title: Tool Contracts
rendered_sections.content:
- graph.describe@v1: Payload with selector string
- graph.find@v1: Payload with optional node_class, name_regex, path_regex, logical_key_regex, exported, case_sensitive, and limit
- graph.path@v1: Payload with start selector, end selector, and optional max_hops
- graph.resolve@v1: Payload with selector string
- run_python_query@v1: Payload with code string plus optional bindings, include_export, export_kwargs, and limits
- session.add@v1: Payload with target selector and optional detail of skeleton|summary|neighborhood|source
- session.collapse@v1: Payload with optional target selector and include_descendants boolean.
- session.estimate_expand@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode, depth, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.estimate_hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.event_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.expand_dependencies@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_dependents@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_file@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.explain_export_omission@v1: Payload with optional target selector plus export tuning fields like max_tokens, compact, visible_levels, and class filters.
- session.export@v1: Payload with optional max_tokens, compact, include_rendered, visible_levels, only_node_classes, exclude_node_classes, max_frontier_actions, and max_rendered_bytes
- session.focus@v1: Payload with optional target selector; null or omission clears focus
- session.hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.hydrate_source@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets.
- session.mutation_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.pin@v1: Payload with optional target selector and pinned boolean.
- session.prune@v1: Payload with optional max_selected integer.
- session.recommendations@v1: Payload with optional top integer
- session.walk@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode=file|dependencies|dependents, depth, limit, relation, relations, priority_threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.why@v1: Payload with target selector
- session.why_pruned@v1: Payload with optional target selector.

entries.rendered_prompt:
## Base Runtime Instructions
GraphBench runtime. Stay bounded, typed, and evidence-oriented.

## UCL Command Reference

## Rules
1. Output ONLY the UCL command(s), no explanations or markdown
2. Use exact block IDs as provided
3. String values must be quoted with double quotes
4. Block IDs are short numeric IDs (1, 2, 3, etc.)
5. Do NOT use operators like + for string concatenation - just provide the full value
6. Responses must be schema-valid.

## Response Contract
Return JSON only with this exact shape: {{"kind":"think|tool_call|complete","assistant_message":"<brief status>","tool_call":{{"tool_name":"<one registered tool name>","payload":{{}}}},"acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"],"readiness_state":"not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit"}}. Use null or omit tool_call unless kind is tool_call. When kind is tool_call, tool_call.tool_name must exactly match one of the registered tool names shown below. Do not invent helper tools, verifier tools, or meta tools.

Task: Gather the evidence required to edit fixture loading while preserving pinned commit validation, local path resolution, and graph snapshot identity checks.
Class: prepare_to_edit

## Tool Contracts
- graph.describe@v1: Payload with selector string
- graph.find@v1: Payload with optional node_class, name_regex, path_regex, logical_key_regex, exported, case_sensitive, and limit
- graph.path@v1: Payload with start selector, end selector, and optional max_hops
- graph.resolve@v1: Payload with selector string
- run_python_query@v1: Payload with code string plus optional bindings, include_export, export_kwargs, and limits
- session.add@v1: Payload with target selector and optional detail of skeleton|summary|neighborhood|source
- session.collapse@v1: Payload with optional target selector and include_descendants boolean.
- session.estimate_expand@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode, depth, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.estimate_hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.event_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.expand_dependencies@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_dependents@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.expand_file@v1: Payload with target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, and traversal budgets.
- session.explain_export_omission@v1: Payload with optional target selector plus export tuning fields like max_tokens, compact, visible_levels, and class filters.
- session.export@v1: Payload with optional max_tokens, compact, include_rendered, visible_levels, only_node_classes, exclude_node_classes, max_frontier_actions, and max_rendered_bytes
- session.focus@v1: Payload with optional target selector; null or omission clears focus
- session.hydrate@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets
- session.hydrate_source@v1: Payload with target selector, optional padding, and hydration budgets.
- session.mutation_log@v1: Payload object with no required fields
- session.pin@v1: Payload with optional target selector and pinned boolean.
- session.prune@v1: Payload with optional max_selected integer.
- session.recommendations@v1: Payload with optional top integer
- session.walk@v1: Payload with target selector, optional mode=file|dependencies|dependents, depth, limit, relation, relations, priority_threshold, and traversal budgets
- session.why@v1: Payload with target selector
- session.why_pruned@v1: Payload with optional target selector.
